---
title: "Bio"
---

![](profile.png){fig-align="center" width="230"}

### Anuththara Rupasinghe, Ph.D. {.bio-name}

<p class="bio-position">Postdoctoral Research Fellow, Princeton University</p>

**Machine Learning | Statistical Signal Processing | Data Science | Computational Neuroscience | Biomedical AI**

I am a machine learning and computational neuroscience researcher with a **Ph.D. in Electrical and Computer Engineering** and postdoctoral training at the Princeton Neuroscience Institute. My work focuses on developing statistical and machine learning methods for analyzing complex neural and biological data, with applications to neural decoding, time-series analysis, uncertainty quantification, and biomedical data science.

My path has been shaped by a long-standing interest in mathematics, engineering, and data-driven scientific discovery. During my undergraduate studies in Electrical and Electronic Engineering at the University of Peradeniya, Sri Lanka, I received the **Best Performance in Engineering** award, along with several prizes in electrical engineering, engineering mathematics, communications, and power systems.

I then joined the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering at the University of Maryland, College Park, supported by the **Clark School of Engineering Distinguished Graduate Fellowship**. There, I completed my Ph.D. under the supervision of Professor [Behtash Babadi](https://ece.umd.edu/clark/faculty/356/Behtash-Babadi), developing Bayesian methods for neural and physiological time-series analysis. In 2022, I was selected as a **Rising Star in EECS**.

Since graduating in 2022, I have been a postdoctoral research fellow at Princeton University, working with Professor [Jonathan Pillow](https://pillowlab.princeton.edu/). At Princeton, I develop machine learning and statistical modeling approaches for understanding neural population activity and behavior. My postdoctoral work has been recognized by the **Princeton Neuroscience Institute QCN T32 Postdoctoral Fellowship Award**.

Across my research, I am interested in using **statistical modeling and machine learning** to extract meaningful structure from **high-dimensional, noisy, and temporally structured data**. I am currently exploring research scientist, applied scientist, machine learning, data science, computational neuroscience, and biomedical AI roles where I can contribute to **method development, data analysis, and interdisciplinary applications**.

## Research interests

- Machine learning
- Bayesian inference
- Predictive modeling
- Data science
- Time-series analysis
- Statistical signal processing
- Computational neuroscience
- Biomedical AI
- Uncertainty quantification

## Selected Awards & Honors

- **Princeton Neuroscience Institute QCN T32 Postdoctoral Fellowship Award**, 2024
- **SfN Trainee Professional Development Award**, 2024
- **Rising Stars in EECS Award**, 2022
- **Outstanding Teaching Assistant**, Department of Electrical & Computer Engineering, University of Maryland, 2018
- **Clark School of Engineering Distinguished Graduate Fellowship**, University of Maryland, 2017
- **Prizes awarded at the General Convocation, University of Peradeniya, Sri Lanka**, 2016
  - C. A. Hewavitharana Prize for the **Best Performance in Engineering**
  - Ceylon Electricity Board Gold Medal and Prize for the **Best Performance in Electrical & Electronic Engineering**
  - E. F. Bartholomeusz Prize for the **Best Performance in Engineering Mathematics**
  - W. M. G. Fernando Prize for the **Best Performance in Electronic Communications**
  - R. H. Paul Prize for the **Best Performance in Electrical Power and Machines**
- **Most Outstanding Performance of the Year**, Mahamaya Girls’ College, Kandy, Sri Lanka, 2011
- **Ranked 4th in Sri Lanka**, G.C.E. Advanced Level Examination, Physical Science stream, 2011
- **Ranked 9th in Sri Lanka**, Olympiad Mathematics Competition, 2011

## Contact

**Email:** [ar0621@princeton.edu](mailto:ar0621@princeton.edu)  
**LinkedIn:** [Anuththara Rupasinghe](https://www.linkedin.com/in/anuththara-rupasinghe-b1566b122/)  
**Google Scholar:** [Google Scholar Profile](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=6TQuQqsAAAAJ&hl=en)  
**GitHub:** [Anuththara-Rupasinghe](https://github.com/Anuththara-Rupasinghe)
